title: Peptide-Centric Local Stability Assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1a7a7","text":"High-throughput Proteome-wide Limited Proteolysis Assay (HT-PELSA) is a mass spectrometry–based method for detecting protein–ligand interactions through ligand-induced changes in protease susceptibility. This protocol describes an optimized workflow for applying HT-PELSA to mammalian cell lysates, based on implementation in K562 cells. Cells are lysed under mild, detergent-free conditions using repeated freeze–thaw cycles in phosphate-buffered saline supplemented with protease inhibitors, minimizing viscosity and preserving native protein conformations. Following normalization of protein concentration, lysates are incubated with small-molecule ligands under controlled conditions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ci84b","text":"The workflow is adapted for high-throughput processing in a 96-well format, where samples are subjected to rapid, time-resolved trypsin digestion. Proteolysis is quenched with denaturant, followed by reduction, alkylation, and acidification. Peptides are subsequently purified using C18 solid-phase extraction in a plate-based format compatible with downstream liquid chromatography–mass spectrometry (LC–MS) analysis. Inclusion of matched vehicle controls and post-quench ligand supplementation enables correction for potential ionization artifacts.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b5pec","text":"This protocol enables scalable and reproducible profiling of proteome-wide ligand binding events in mammalian systems. The streamlined format reduces handling time and sample variability, making it suitable for compound screening, target engagement studies, and systems-level analysis of protein stability and conformational changes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Pellet cells at 1,000 × g for 5 min
Prepare lysis buffer: PBS + 1% (v/v) protease inhibitor cocktail (Sigma P8340)
Resuspend cells in ice-cold lysis buffer
Option A (cleared lysate): centrifuge at 20,000 × g for 10 min at 4 °C
Option B (crude lysate): skip centrifugation and use directly
Measure protein concentration (e.g., Rapid Gold BCA)
Adjust lysate to 1.2 mg/mL using lysis buffer
Prepare ligand stocks at 100× in DMSO
Prepare control samples with matched DMSO volume
Split each sample into four 50 µL replicates in a 96-well plate
Prepare second 96-well plate with 5 µL trypsin (5 mg/mL; Sigma T1426) per well
Keep trypsin on ice until use
Transfer 50 µL sample into trypsin-containing wells
Mix by pipetting up/down for 30 s
Add 165 µL of 8 M guanidine HCl + 60 mM HEPES pH 8.2 (3× volume)
Add ligand to control samples to match treated samples
Add TCEP to 10 mM final concentration
Add chloroacetamide (CAA) to 40 mM final concentration
Spin empty plate at 3,000 × g for 5 min
Wash 2× with 1 mL 100% ACN (20 × g, 1 min each)
Wash 2× with 1 mL 0.1% TFA (100 × g, 1 min, accel 5/9)
Wash 2× with 1 mL 0.1% TFA (same spin settings)
Elute 2× with 100 µL 50% ACN + 0.1% TFA (20 × g, 1 min each)
Use spacer to prevent tips touching collection plate during elution
Wash and scrape or pellet adherent cells
Apply same PBS + 1% protease inhibitor lysis
Proceed with protocol unchanged after normalization to 1.2 mg/mL
creator: Ayush Regmi Bagale
